name: Middle Dutch (ca. 1050-1350)

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Middle Dutch (midd1321).

notes: Skeleton. West Germanic (Low Franconian) medieval stage written in the Latin alphabet. Grapheme map covers the monophthong letters, the digraph long vowels (ae/ee/oo/oe/ie/uu) and diphthongs (ij/ou/au/ei), the fricative digraphs sch and ch, and the plain consonants including g [ɣ] and w [ʋ]. Middle Dutch spelling varied widely by scribe and region; vowel length was written inconsistently and reduced/schwa realisations of e are not recoverable, so these are left unencoded and a real PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Middle Dutch (midd1321)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
